docs.title: Rohith Kumar Prasanna Kumar
docs.text:
Award-Winning Software Engineer | Cybersecurity & Network Systems Specialist | Intel Division Recognition Award Winner

📍 Gdańsk, Poland | 📞 +48 881 046 707 | ✉️ rkrohithkumar@gmail.com

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I thrive on tackling tough engineering problems where hardware meets software—whether that's designing 25.6 Tbps programmable Ethernet switches at Intel or building cloud security defenses at WithSecure.

What gets me up in the morning: Turning "impossible" challenges into clean, working solutions. At Intel, I fixed those nasty PHV bugs that snagged me their Division Recognition Award (their second-highest honor). Now at WithSecure, I'm crafting AI-driven scalable cloud testing frameworks that keep enterprises safe worldwide.

docs.title: My Engineering Toolkit
docs.text:
Category Technologies 🚀 Cloud & Cybersecurity AWS, Azure, Docker, AI-driven test frameworks ⚡ Network Systems ASIC/SoC, P4 switches, Tofino family (12.8–25.6 Tbps) 🔬 Test Automation Python, Selenium, Pytest, Robot Framework 🏎️ Autonomous Systems BMW MPAD & HPAD, PSA-CSLP, CAN, Vector Tools 💡 Entrepreneurship Co-founded Bionix Division

Tech stack I live in: Python | C/C++ | Linux | P4 | AWS | Azure | Docker | Selenium | LabVIEW | ASIC Design

docs.title: Where I Am
docs.text: I call Gdańsk, Poland home but work remotely with my awesome Helsinki-based team at WithSecure, blending 9+ years across cybersecurity, networking, embedded systems, and test engineering. Always mentoring folks globally too.

docs.title: Experience Snapshot
docs.text:
Period Company Role 2022 – Present WithSecure Senior Software Engineer in Test 2021 – 2022 Intel ASIC Design Engineer (Division Award) 2019 – 2020 Aptiv Test Team Lead 2018 – 2019 UST Global Associate Developer (Intel Client) 2017 Jabil Test Engineer 2013 – 2015 Bionix Division Co-Founder

View my complete CV →

docs.text: Award-Winning Software Engineer who thrives on tough engineering problems where hardware meets software. Intel Division Recognition Award winner. 9+ years across cybersecurity, networking, embedded systems, and test engineering.

docs.title: WithSecure (07.2022 – Present)
docs.text:
Senior Software Engineer in Test – Cloud Solution

  • End-to-end testing of corporate solutions, exchange, SharePoint protection
  • Network modelling and anomaly detection (AI Modelling)
  • Test online security, software-updater, browser protection (OPSWAT) – Docker
  • Create VMs on Azure and VMware vSphere for automated testing
  • Test domains for safe/unsafe environments (AWS, S3, CloudFront, Route53, EC2)
  • Python test automation, CI/CD maintenance (Jenkins)

Environment: Python, Selenium, Docker, Pytest, Azure, vSphere, AWS, S3, CloudFront, Route53, EC2, Jira, Jenkins

docs.title: Intel Technologies Poland (01.2021 – 06.2022)
docs.text:
ASIC SoC Design Engineer – Programmable Network Switches

  • Design verification of Ethernet switches on model, emulator (Veloc-Strato+), and silicon
  • Hitless Warm Reboot (zero packet loss) on Tofino switching ASIC
  • Tested P4C compiler with complex P4 programs (parser stress, fuzzer, FIFO occupancy)
  • Tested low-level device drivers (PCIe, DMA), forwarding pipeline, packet parser (Tcams)
  • Mentored cross-geo development teams
  • Products: Tofino1, Tofino2 (12.8 Tbps), Tofino3 (25.6 Tbps), Tofino5 (Flat-rock)

Achievements: Intel Division Recognition Award (PHV bug fix, bit flip) + 2x Intel Recognition Award

Environment: Python (PTF), Git, C++, Linux, Scapy, IXIA, Wireshark, GDB, P4, P4C, gcc, Veloc-Strato+, EPGM, JIRA

docs.title: Aptiv Services Poland (09.2019 – 12.2020)
docs.text:
Manufacturing Test Engineer – Test Team Lead

  • Test Team Lead – Peugeot Autonomous Cars Project
  • Test Support Engineer – BMW Autonomous Cars Project
  • Functional test (Visual Basic, C#, LabVIEW, TestStand)
  • IATF Standard, ISO 26262 compliance
  • Communication modules: Ethernet switches (NXP, Broadcom, Marvell, Microchip), CAN (Vector Tools), SPI, I2C
  • Stress testing (Nping, TRex)
  • Microcontrollers/SoCs: Infineon Aurix, Intel EyeQ, Denveron
  • Cybersecurity: Kali Linux, exploitation testing

Projects: BMW – Camera modules & communication | Peugeot – Radar & camera systems

Environment: C#, Python, LabVIEW, TestStand, Visual Basic, Excel, Kali Linux, Nping, Git, Plastic SCM, Polarion, Doors, CANoe, Vector Tools, CAN, SPI, I2C

Projects: - Gesture controlled robotic vehicles with video surveillance - Robotic arm using EMG signals (Embedded C, MATLAB) - PCB design (Eagle, Diptrace) - Brain wave-controlled wheelchair (Arduino, ZigBee, C++) - EEG signal-based home automation (Arduino, C++)
